design_philosophy.core_loop: Traverse levels while collecting power-ups (weapons, bird companion, spear); defeat boss enemies to progress through themed stages
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management (health bar refills, limited weapons/tools) combined with pattern recognition for boss encounters
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Embody Sharaku, the three-eyed superhuman protagonist, solving mysteries and battling enemies across exotic global locations (Easter Island, etc.) from the Osamu Tezuka manga
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Game maintains manga narrative structure with mandatory cutscenes between levels showing defeated bosses and story progression; heavily dependent on source material IP rather than original game design innovation

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Debug mode allows skipping levels, refilling health, and acquiring all weapons/companions, suggesting developers anticipated difficulty friction or testing burden

progression.upgrade_system: Weapons collection (spear variants), bird companion acquisition, monetary currency system (enemies drop money in 1 and 10 increments for debug purposes)
progression.unlock_mechanism: Boss defeat gates progression to next stage; hidden stage select available via cheat code (A+B + specific D-Pad sequence)
replayability_hooks: Hidden level select for non-linear progression
Multiple weapon/tool combinations to experiment with
Stage-based structure allows individual level replay

world_building_techniques: Mandatory post-boss cutscenes displaying defeated boss artwork with Japanese text narration
Exotic real-world location theming (Easter Island with Moai statues, implied Americas/Mexico stages)
Character relationships maintained from manga (Sharaku as protagonist, companion character Chiyoko Wato presence implied)
Stage structure mirrors manga mystery-investigation format

boss_design.philosophy: One boss per stage; boss defeat directly gates narrative progression via cutscene; manga-derived boss designs reflecting story villains and mysteries
notable_bosses: Stage 4 boss (final cutscene available, but stage 5 has no cutscene despite unused data existing)
Moai statues (implied stage 5 boss based on unused Easter Island cutscene text referencing statues 'mounting attack')
Unknown 'Sodom' sprite exists as unused asset, suggesting cut boss encounter

faction_indicators: Three-eyed civilization as ancient superpower faction
Modern human society unaware of three-eyed remnants (Sharaku hidden by cross-bandage)
danger_signals: Sharaku's suppressed third eye as latent threat when bandage removed
Ancient ruins and civilization technology as dangerous artifacts
Boss encounters as manifestations of civilization mysteries
environmental_storytelling: Easter Island Moai statues as environmental boss arena
Global location progression (Arizona, Easter Island, Mexico) suggesting world-spanning mystery
Cutscene imagery of defeated bosses reinforces narrative stakes

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Mitsume ga Tooru NES technical documentation)
